description: An Italian antipasto of grilled or toasted bread rubbed with garlic and topped with olive oil and various ingredients. The common tomato version usually includes chopped tomatoes, basil, and sometimes mozzarella or balsamic glaze.
preparation_steps: Slice the bread and toast or grill it until crisp on the outside.
Rub the warm bread lightly with garlic and drizzle with olive oil.
Prepare the topping, such as chopped tomatoes with basil and seasoning.
Spoon the topping over the bread shortly before serving.

nutrition.calories: Usually low to moderate per piece
nutrition.protein: Low; higher if cheese or cured meat is added
nutrition.fat: Low to moderate, mainly from olive oil

health_advice.benefits: Can be a relatively light appetizer and may include heart-healthy olive oil and fresh vegetables.
health_advice.risks: Portion size can increase quickly, and sodium may rise if cheese, cured meat, or salted toppings are used.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Use quality bread, moderate olive oil, and plenty of fresh tomato or vegetable topping.

dietary_notes.note: Traditional bread-based bruschetta is not gluten-free unless made with gluten-free bread. Vegan versions are easy when cheese is omitted.

allergens: Gluten from bread unless gluten-free bread is used
